qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Prototype pollution via unvalidated object property keys in parse()
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable function (parse/resolver in flatted), same root cause (unvalidated property values used as array indices without numeric coercion, allowing __proto__ to access Array.prototype via the prototype chain), same CWE-1321, same affected component (flatted < 3.4.2). The finding described Object.prototype pollution via __proto__ key assignment while the advisory describes Array.prototype leak via __proto__ value-as-index, but both stem from the same missing validation in the resolver function at cjs/index.js:30.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Resource exhaustion via uncontrolled recursion in parse()
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerability class (resource exhaustion/DoS vs prototype pollution), different root cause (unbounded recursion vs unvalidated property access as array index), and different security consequence (CPU consumption vs prototype chain pollution).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Type confusion via String object wrapping in parse()
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerability class (type confusion vs prototype pollution), different root cause (String object wrapping/unwrapping vs unvalidated property access as array index), and different security consequence (typeof/strict equality bypass vs prototype chain pollution).

selected_finding.title: Prototype pollution via unvalidated object property keys in parse()

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The finding identifies the same prototype pollution vulnerability in flatted parse() described in the advisory. The advisory root cause (using __proto__ as an unvalidated array index into the input buffer at cjs/index.js:30, leaking Array.prototype) was confirmed via PoC execution. The PoC demonstrated that parse('[{"x":"__proto__"}]') returns an object where parsed.x === Array.prototype, and writing to parsed.x pollutes Array.prototype globally, confirmed by [].polluted_advisory === 'pwned'.
evidence: PoC run.log line 8: parsed.x === Array.prototype: true
PoC run.log line 10: EXPLOIT SUCCESS: Array.prototype polluted via advisory mechanism
Advisory root cause (input[value] without +coercion at cjs/index.js:30) confirmed exploitable after reverting the v3.4.2 fix
Same CWE-1321 (Prototype Pollution), same affected function (parse/resolver), same affected component (flatted < 3.4.2)

poc_executions.observed_effect: Parsed '[{"x":"__proto__"}]' via flatted.parse(). Result property parsed.x === Array.prototype confirmed. Writing parsed.x.polluted_advisory = 'pwned' polluted Array.prototype globally, verified by [].polluted_advisory === 'pwned'.
